title: The Flow of E-Waste Pollutants into Oceanic Ecosystems
description: A visual map tracing the journey of hazardous materials from discarded electronics through various environmental pathways, culminating in their impact on marine life and food webs.
relationships: Improper disposal leads to leaching of toxins into soil and groundwater
Contaminated water and soil runoff transports pollutants into rivers and estuaries
Rivers carry pollutants into coastal marine environments
Pollutants bioaccumulate in marine organisms and biomagnify up the food chain
Humans are exposed by consuming contaminated seafood
